message:
Migrate event storage from rusqlite to fsqlite API

Replace rusqlite Transaction-based event operations with the fsqlite
Connection API. This continues the broader migration from rusqlite to
the frankenSQLite engine across the codebase:

- Replace `rusqlite::{Connection, Transaction, params}` imports with
  `fsqlite::{Connection, Row}` and `fsqlite_types::SqliteValue`
- Change `insert_event` to accept `&Connection` instead of `&Transaction<'_>`,
  as fsqlite manages transaction boundaries via explicit BEGIN/COMMIT on
  the connection rather than separate transaction objects
- Convert parameterized query from `params![]` macro to explicit
  `SqliteValue` array with manual `Option` → `SqliteValue::Null` mapping
- Replace `tx.last_insert_rowid()` with a `SELECT last_insert_rowid()`
  query through the fsqlite row API
- Update all downstream callers (`insert_created_event`, `insert_updated_event`,
  etc.) to pass `&Connection` instead of `&Transaction<'_>`
- Update `query_events` and `count_events` to use fsqlite row access
  patterns (`.get(N).and_then(|v| v.as_text())`) instead of rusqlite's
  typed `row.get()` calls
- Fix DateTime parsing to handle fsqlite's string-based return values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
